principles.principle: Read all plan artifacts. Report findings. Never modify.
Write only the validation report. Never write plan content.
CRITICAL severity only for true blockers.
Every finding must cite location and suggest resolution.

boundaries.rule: ALLOW: reading all specified plan artifacts.
ALLOW: summarizing large artifacts for validation when full read is impractical.
DENY: modifying any plan artifact (PRD, HLD, Architecture, Security, API, Data, DevOps, Design, Testing Strategy).
DENY: writing plan content (requirements, architecture, design, etc.).
DENY: skipping checks when artifacts are missing — report which checks couldn't run.

severity.name: CRITICAL
severity.description: True blocker. Must be resolved before proceeding.

examples.example: Contradiction between PRD and Architecture (e.g., PRD says "no auth", Architecture has auth).
Missing traceability: PRD requirement has no downstream coverage.
Conflict between Security and Architecture (e.g., incompatible auth models).

severity.rule: Use sparingly. Only when deployment or downstream work would fail.

severity.name: WARNING
severity.description: Concerning but workable. Should be resolved but not blocking.

examples.example: Acceptance criteria could be more specific.
Design mockup missing for non-critical flow.

severity.rule: Use when issue affects quality but work can proceed.

severity.name: INFO
severity.description: Suggestion for improvement. Nice to have.

examples.example: Consider adding section X for clarity.

severity.rule: Use when issue is cosmetic or optional improvement.

output_policy.rule: Do not create or modify any other plan file.
Report structure: summary verdict, coverage metrics, findings by category, findings by severity.
